title: Crispy Kenyan Beef Roast
description: Savory, crispy, nostalgic Kenyan dish featuring beef shin with a flavorful bhuna rub, eggfree-friendly and perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Pat beef shin dry with paper towels.
In a bowl, mix paprika, cumin, garlic powder, onion powder, chili flakes, salt, and pepper.
Rub mixture all over the beef shin, pressing into the meat, and let rest at room temperature for 15 minutes to absorb flavors.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat; sear the beef shin on all sides until browned (about 5 minutes). Transfer to a roasting pan with a rack if available.
Pour 1 cup of beef broth into the bottom of the roasting pan and add a tablespoon of lemon juice to help tenderize.
Cover tightly with foil (or a lid) and roast in the oven for 2 hours.

Remove the cover, increase oven temperature to 230°C (450°F) or switch to broiler, and roast for an additional 30 minutes, basting with broth occasionally, until the skin is crisp and edges are browned.
Let the roast rest for 10–15 minutes before slicing to allow juices to redistribute.
Serve slices with a side of sautéed greens or steamed greens, and drizzle pan juices over the meat.
Enjoy the nostalgic, comforting Kenyan beef roast.
